SourceProxy.name: SHYXOppositeFaceThickness
SourceProxy.label: SHYX Opposite Face Thickness

Documentation.short_help: Per-point thickness along -normal; optional thin-shell recast after +normal push.

SourceProxy.Documentation: For each point, casts a ray along the direction opposite the point normal (inward for an outward-oriented surface), finds the first intersected triangle that does not use the query vertex, and stores the Euclidean distance from the query point to the hit in point data (default array name OppositeFaceThickness). With Recalculate enabled, vertices whose first thickness is below ThinThicknessRecalculateThreshold are moved on the output mesh along +normal by that distance, then thickness is recomputed from the new position when possible. Uses vtkStaticCellLocator and vtkSMPTools. If normals are missing, optional vtkPolyDataNormals can be run when AutoComputeNormals is on. No hit yields NaN in the output array.

IntVectorProperty.name: AutoComputeNormals
IntVectorProperty.label: Auto Compute Normals
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When on, point normals are computed with vtkPolyDataNormals (Normals on the pipeline output). When off, choose a point vector array below.

StringVectorProperty.name: NormalArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Normal Array
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: 3-component point vectors used as outward normals when Auto Compute Normals is off.

IntVectorProperty.name: ThinThicknessRecalculate
IntVectorProperty.label: Recalculate If Thinner Than Threshold
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When on, output points are deep-copied from the input. For each vertex whose first thickness is finite, non-negative, and strictly less than ThinThicknessRecalculateThreshold, the output position becomes p + threshold * (+normal); thickness is then recomputed from that position (same ray rules). The stored thickness uses the second pass when it is finite, otherwise the first pass value. Requires a positive ThinThicknessRecalculateThreshold.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: ThinThicknessRecalculateThreshold
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Recalculate threshold (compare & +normal push)
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Used only with Recalculate If Thinner Than Threshold: the same number is (1) the strict upper bound on the first-pass thickness that triggers a second pass, and (2) the distance the query point is shifted along +normal before that second ray cast. Must be > 0.
